activeGoal: Fast-track implementation of the human-architect-approved Neo4j chain: add Execute Neo4j Cypher and Neo4j Query Native T
designSummary: Minimal architect repair for QA issue ISSUE-001 on baseline commit 86b3f4501d6a8529b72a736bb18f8dff59d52034. Human-authored architecture remains authoritative and unchanged: ApplicationFunction {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC} Execute Neo4j Cypher stays assigned to existing software unit ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI, and ApplicationComponent {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} Neo4j Query Native Tool remains the agent-facing tool boundary serving that CLI. Minimal rework is limited to compliant task traceability and intent-tagged automated coverage for {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}; no architecture semantics, capability boundaries, or human-architect nodes are redesigned. Because TASK-001 is a planning-only escalation artifact without architectureElementId mapping, downstream implementation should be routed through a new function-level implementation task anchored to software unit ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI and architecture element {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}. No Requirement ID is introduced because none was authoritatively recoverable from the graph.
designDecisions: ADR: Preserve the human-authored Neo4j architecture unchanged for ISSUE-001. The approved ApplicationFunction {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC} and ApplicationComponent {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} remain authoritative; the issue is traceability/task-contract completeness, not a design defect.

ADR: Accept existing software unit ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI (STIX Semantic Query CLI) as the implementation owner for Execute Neo4j Cypher because the realized behavior already exists in agent_app/opencode_app/tools/stix_cli/semantic_query.py and __main__.py, and the graph already contains the Assignment from ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI to {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}.

ADR: Reject introducing a new Neo4j-specific software unit or moving ownership into the tool wrapper. The JS tool file agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/tools/neo4j_query.js correctly remains the agent-facing ApplicationComponent {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394}; it already has tagged tests. The missing QA intent coverage is specifically at ApplicationFunction {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}, so the minimal repair is tagged tests and task routing anchored to the existing Python CLI unit.

ADR: Do not invent or backfill a Requirement ID for the Neo4j batch because no authoritative requirement element was recoverable from the runtime graph. Route implementation rework using architecture-element traceability and preserve the absence of a requirement link as a known upstream metadata gap rather than fabricating motivation semantics.

audit.summary: Architecture compliance audit passed for commit 05d14a419e925bc9e7f59afff983daa3a1c42323 in full-model lane after re-QA. Intent model is sufficient with all core layers present. Reviewed batch is limited to tests/test_stix_cli.py and introduces no architectural code drift. Explicit architecture trace evidence remains present for ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI in agent_app/opencode_app/tools/stix_cli/semantic_query.py, for {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC} in semantic_query.py and tests/test_stix_cli.py, and for {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394} in agent_app/opencode_app/.opencode/tools/neo4j_query.js and tests/test_neo4j_query_tool.py. Reality scan verifiedIntentIds includes ELM-APP-COMP-STIX-CLI, {E1A3F02C-9F97-464c-9247-DE4EBB2BB5CC}, and {1CFA011B-787D-4e43-BE86-0AC04FE53394}, satisfying required automated intent coverage for the Neo4j batch.
